description: Atmospheric horror stories, creepypasta, urban legends, paranormal tales for teens and adults
target_audience: 16-35 year olds who love horror content

narrator.name: Vincent
narrator.personality: calm storyteller who makes horror feel more real through understatement
narrator.voice_style: low, measured, slightly hushed, ominous undertone, like reading by candlelight

visual_style.art_style: dark atmospheric digital art, deep shadows, limited light sources, horror movie aesthetic, painterly, dramatic chiaroscuro lighting
visual_style.color_palette: deep black, cold blue-gray, sickly pale yellow, blood red accents, fog white
visual_style.quality_tags: atmospheric, dark, cinematic, moody, high quality horror art
visual_style.negative_prompt: cartoon, bright colors, cheerful, explicit gore, children, nudity, text

backgrounds.dark_forest: ominous dark forest at night, single path, fog, distant light, deeply unsettling
backgrounds.abandoned_house: abandoned Victorian house, broken windows, overgrown, moonlight, eerie
backgrounds.foggy_road: empty country road in thick fog, single headlight beam, isolation
backgrounds.basement: dark basement, single hanging light bulb, shadows, brick walls, dread
backgrounds.cemetery_night: atmospheric old cemetery at night, moonlight on stones, mist, silence
backgrounds.mirror_dark: dark room with old mirror, subtle wrongness in reflection, psychological horror

content_pillars: The man who lived in my walls for three years
I found my missing sister's diary - I wish I hadn't
Something is wrong with my neighbor
The last voicemail from my dead wife
I work night shift at a rest stop
There is something in the lake behind my house
My daughter has an imaginary friend that knows things
The smile that followed me home
I found a door in my basement that was not there before
The gas station at mile marker 56
llm_system_prompt: You are a master horror story writer in the tradition of creepypasta. Write in first person. Build atmosphere slowly. Use mundane details to make the horror feel real. Never explain everything - ambiguity is scarier. No graphic gore or sexual content. Psychological dread over shock value. Safe rating: PG-13 horror.
music_style: dark ambient, dissonant drones, subtle unsettling tones, horror soundtrack style, tension building, no melody
